ev.headline: Toyota Prius Outselling Every Battery-Electric Vehicle In America but Two
ev.summary: The 2026 Toyota Prius is outselling every EV in America with available sales data, as battery-electric vehicle sales nose-dive amid manufacturer cutbacks and production delays.

ev.headline: JD Power 2026 EV Ownership Study Shows Record Satisfaction Despite Market Volatility
ev.summary: Overall satisfaction among current battery-electric vehicle owners is at its highest level since 2021, with 96% of new BEV owners saying they would consider purchasing another EV for their next vehicle.

ev.headline: Nio Battery-Swap Network Reaches Milestone: 165,000+ Daily Swaps
ev.summary: Nio's battery-swap system in China achieved a new record with more than 165,000 battery swaps in a single day, demonstrating battery swap technology can rival traditional refueling models at scale.

ev.headline: Red E Charge Exceeds 1,500 DC Fast-Charging Ports, Emerging as 7th Largest Network
ev.summary: Red E Charge has expanded to over 1,500 DC fast-charging ports across 436 locations, becoming one of the fastest-growing charging networks in the United States with strong customer satisfaction scores.

ev.headline: Global EV Charging Station Market Projected to Reach $143 Billion by 2035
ev.summary: The global EV charging market is undergoing exponential expansion with rapid deployment of ultra-fast charging, vehicle-to-grid integration, and renewable energy integration driving growth from $26.87 billion in 2025.

ev.headline: NEVI Program Reboot: Federal Guidance Unlocks Billions for EV Charging Infrastructure
ev.summary: The U.S. National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) program officially restarted with updated federal guidance, unlocking billions in previously frozen funds with expanded focus on medium and heavy-duty charging.

ev.headline: Ford Unveils $30,000 Electric Pickup Strategy with New UEV Platform
ev.summary: Ford Motor Company detailed its Universal Electric Vehicle (UEV) platform targeting a mid-size electric pickup at $30,000 for launch in 2027, focusing on affordability through efficiency gains and battery downsizing.

ev.headline: Stellantis Takes $26 Billion Charge, Retreating from EV Strategy Amid Market Pressures
ev.summary: Stellantis disclosed a $26.2 billion write-down tied to overestimating EV adoption, canceling the Ram 1500 REV pickup and scaling back electrification across North American brands as the company shifts toward demand-driven strategy.

ev.headline: Big Three Automakers Write Off $55 Billion from EV Strategy Pivots
ev.summary: Cumulative charges from General Motors ($6.6B), Ford ($19.5B), and Stellantis ($26B) totaling $52.1 billion reflect massive overestimation of EV demand as manufacturers shift focus to hybrids and ICE vehicles.

ev.headline: BYD Overtakes Tesla as World's Largest EV Seller with 2.26 Million Units
ev.summary: Chinese automaker BYD sold 2.26 million EVs in 2025, surpassing Tesla's declining sales of 1.6 million units, marking a historic shift in global EV market leadership despite neither company's presence in America.

ev.headline: CATL to Launch Sodium-Ion Batteries at Scale by End of 2026
ev.summary: Battery leader CATL announced widespread availability of sodium-ion batteries by end of 2026, offering a cheaper alternative to lithium-ion while reducing dependence on lithium resources for cost-sensitive EV segments.

ev.headline: Solid-State Battery Progress: Donut Lab Claims Production Vehicle; Mercedes Tests 745-Mile Range
ev.summary: Donut Lab claims the first solid-state battery in a production vehicle, while Factorial Energy's cells enabled a Mercedes test vehicle to drive 745 miles on a single charge in real-world conditions.

ev.headline: Polestar Announces Four-Model Offensive Through 2028 with $600M in New Funding
ev.summary: Polestar unveiled its largest product offensive since becoming a standalone brand, planning four new vehicles through 2028 including the Polestar 7 compact SUV, backed by $600 million in new equity funding.

ev.headline: Tesla Cybertruck AWD Launch Limited to 10 Days at $59,990
ev.summary: Tesla launched a dual-motor all-wheel-drive Cybertruck starting at $59,990, with CEO Elon Musk signaling the limited offer will expire in just 10 days depending on demand.

ev.headline: Hyundai Ioniq 3 Debuts in 2026 as Europe-Focused Affordable EV
ev.summary: Hyundai is launching the Ioniq 3, a compact affordable EV targeted specifically at Europe to offset tariff pressures on US imports, positioning around €30,000 and competing in the growing value EV segment.

ev.headline: Over 30 New EVs Launching in 2026 After Year of Cancellations and Delays
ev.summary: Nearly 30 new electric models are slated to arrive in the U.S. in 2026, representing the first generation of software-defined vehicles built on all-new architectures with true mass-market pricing.

ev.headline: US EV Fast-Charging Network Grew Over 30% Despite Sales Slump
ev.summary: The U.S. fast-charging infrastructure expanded significantly with over 30% growth last year, even as electric vehicle sales declined following the expiration of federal tax credits.

ev.headline: California Proposes $200 Million EV Rebate Program with Automaker Cost-Sharing
ev.summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom proposed a $200 million EV incentive program requiring automakers to match contributions, targeting first-time buyers and addressing the loss of federal tax credits.

ev.headline: All 50 US States Now Have EV Support Policies, EDF Report Shows
ev.summary: Environmental Defense Fund analysis reveals all 50 states have adopted multiple EV-supportive policies including charging infrastructure incentives, purchase rebates, and fleet electrification programs.

ev.headline: BYD's $14,000 Electric SUV Sparks 'Extinction-Level Event' Warning from US Automakers
ev.summary: US automakers warn BYD's newly unveiled fully electric crossover SUV priced at just $14,000 could be an 'extinction-level event' for American automakers, highlighting massive competitive gap with Chinese EVs.

ev.headline: Ford Explores Potential Partnership with China's Geely Amid EV Strategy Shift
ev.summary: Ford and China's Geely are reportedly in discussions about a potential partnership as Ford seeks to access Chinese manufacturing competence and scale amid ongoing EV strategy recalibration.

ev.headline: Toyota Plans Solid-State Battery Production Launch in 2026
ev.summary: Toyota acquired its battery joint venture with Panasonic and plans to begin producing solid-state batteries in 2026, targeting extended range and faster charging compared to traditional lithium-ion cells.

ev.headline: EPA Rollback of Biden EV Mandate Signals Major Policy Shift in 2026
ev.summary: President Trump's EPA has initiated steps to terminate Biden's EV mandate targeting 56% of vehicle sales by 2032, rolling back emission standards and shifting regulatory environment significantly.

energy.headline: SEIA Reports 2025 Battery Storage Growth of 29%, Behind-the-Meter Market Surges as Federal Tax Credits End
energy.summary: Battery energy storage installations surpassed 57 GWh/28 GW in 2025 with a 29% year-over-year increase, while the residential behind-the-meter market showed strong growth as the 25D tax credit expired at year-end.

energy.headline: California Heat Pump Adoption Faces Challenge from High Electricity Rates Despite Electrification Goals
energy.summary: California aims to install 6 million heat pumps by 2030 to reduce emissions, but some of the nation's highest electricity rates stand in the way, making combined solar and heat pump systems increasingly attractive.

energy.headline: UCSB Scientists Develop Molecular Solar Thermal Battery for Residential Water Heating and Energy Storage
energy.summary: Researchers at UC Santa Barbara created a new material using pyrimidone that captures sunlight and stores it as heat on demand, potentially revolutionizing residential solar thermal applications without traditional batteries.

energy.headline: Syntropic Power Launches Sodium-Ion Battery Products to Challenge Tesla Powerwall for Residential Energy Storage
energy.summary: North Carolina-based Syntropic announced three new sodium-ion battery products for residential, commercial, and industrial storage with improved safety and cost advantages over lithium-ion alternatives.

energy.headline: Lunar Energy Secures $232 Million in Series C and D Funding for AI-Powered Residential Energy Storage Expansion
energy.summary: Lunar Energy raised $232 million with backing from Sunrun to expand its AI-powered home battery system management platform across multiple U.S. markets.

energy.headline: Battery Storage Costs Hit Record Lows as BloombergNEF Reports 27% Year-on-Year Decline in 2025
energy.summary: Global benchmark costs for four-hour battery projects fell 27% year-on-year to $78/MWh in 2025, while developers added 87 gigawatts of combined solar and storage delivering power at $57/MWh average.

energy.headline: Federal Solar Tax Credit Expires: Residential Solar Systems Must Be Installed by December 31, 2025
energy.summary: The 30% Residential Clean Energy Credit for solar panels and batteries expired on December 31, 2025, eliminating the primary federal incentive for home solar adoption that had been in place for two decades.

energy.headline: Post-Federal Credit Era: States Step Up with Solar and Heat Pump Incentives for 2026-2027
energy.summary: With federal residential tax credits expiring, states including California, Arizona, North Carolina, and Colorado are implementing new incentive programs through state tax credits, rebates, and utility programs.

energy.headline: Wisconsin Doubles Rooftop Solar Rebates to $600/kW, Maximum $2,400 per System in Response to Federal Credit Cuts
energy.summary: Focus on Energy increased its rooftop solar rebates from $300 to $600 per kilowatt to offset the elimination of federal tax credits for residential solar installations.

energy.headline: Tariffs and Manufacturing Mandates Will Add Costs to Solar Panels and Heat Pumps in 2026
energy.summary: New domestic manufacturing requirements took effect January 1, 2026, and tariffs on imported solar components from China and Vietnam will likely increase residential system costs and extend payback periods.

energy.headline: Home Energy Storage Costs Decline Significantly in 2026 Despite End of Federal Tax Credits
energy.summary: While the 30% federal tax credit expired, battery pack costs are declining to $108/kWh with further 3% declines expected in 2026, making multi-day backup power storage cost-effective for homeowners.

energy.headline: Virtual Power Plants Emerge as Solution to Meet Rising Electricity Demand and Grid Reliability Challenges
energy.summary: Utilities increasingly embrace networks of rooftop solar, backup batteries, electric vehicles, and smart thermostats as virtual power plants to provide grid flexibility and manage unprecedented electricity demand growth.

energy.headline: 60% of U.S. Households Can Save Money and Weather Blackouts with Solar-Plus-Storage Systems
energy.summary: Stanford research shows about 60% of American families could reduce electricity costs by 15% on average by installing solar-battery systems, though benefits are not evenly distributed across regions.

energy.headline: California Solar-Plus-Storage Installations Surge as NEM 3.0 Creates Economic Incentives for Battery Adoption
energy.summary: Solar paired with battery installations make up about 9% of California's residential net metering capacity, with over 40,000 new installations added between October 2023 and April 2024, driven by lower export credits.

energy.headline: NEM 3.0 Makes Battery Storage Worth the Investment for Most California Homeowners in 2026
energy.summary: Battery storage achieves 4-8 year payback in California under NEM 3.0 through time-of-use arbitrage, with high electricity users seeing returns under 5 years despite the 75% reduction in export credits.

energy.headline: Half of U.S. Homes Cannot Support Rooftop Solar; Community Solar Programs Expand Access
energy.summary: Nearly 50% of American homes are unable to support rooftop solar installations, making community solar and virtual power plants increasingly important for widespread solar adoption and energy independence.

energy.headline: Net Metering Policy Revisions Accelerate Across 47 States in Q1 2025
energy.summary: About one-third of U.S. states are offering alternatives to traditional net metering or making significant revisions, with states iterating on net metering successors and expanding system size limits.

energy.headline: California Assembly Bill 942 Would Retroactively Reduce Solar Incentives for Legacy NEM Contracts
energy.summary: Proposed legislation would slash financial incentives for existing rooftop solar customers across California's three NEM tiers, potentially reducing home values and solar installation values despite grandfathering provisions.

energy.headline: Retrofit Solar Battery Storage Emerges as $30B+ Market Opportunity for Installers
energy.summary: Lawrence Berkeley Lab estimates fewer than 10% of residential solar systems installed before 2020 included batteries, creating a large untapped market for retrofitting existing solar-only homes with storage.

energy.headline: Residential Battery Market Expected to Surge to $328.7 Billion by 2035 at 21.6% CAGR
energy.summary: Global residential battery market is experiencing rapid growth with Asia-Pacific projected as fastest-growing region, driven by rising electricity demand, rooftop solar installations, and government renewable incentives.
